target: data/predictions.json — two entries (PRED-SR-SUPPRESS, PRED-093) resolving ISS-1489 (c6 H1 moderate: structured fields contradict detail_reasoning prose for both entries). Flip data_predates_registration from false to true and is_genuinely_prospective from true to false for both entries, to align the structured fields with the detail_reasoning prose argument that the March 2026-03-20–22 G3 geomagnetic storm Schumann-resonance data was available to the author before registration of these predictions. Append a dated reclassification note to the notes field of each entry so the audit trail is preserved (the prior 2026-04-10 decider classification of "genuinely prospective" is retained verbatim above the new note, not deleted). No edits to claim, detail_reasoning, tldr, dome_confidence, category, test_window, or any other field.
patch_instructions: Four atomic edits on data/predictions.json — for each of the two entries (PRED-SR-SUPPRESS and PRED-093), flip data_predates_registration false→true, flip is_genuinely_prospective true→false, and replace the notes field with the audit-trailed version (PATCH-C and PATCH-D below). The notes replacements are done via string substitution on the full BEFORE notes field (both verified globally unique across every stringly field of every predictions.json entry on 2026-04-23). The boolean flips (PATCH-A and PATCH-B) are direct object-property writes targeted by entry id. After integration: (a) run tests; (b) verify build-scripts/generate-html.js _splitPredictions() now places both entries in the MINED bucket (not TOMBSTONE) — this is an intentional visible rendering change and the point of ISS-1489; (c) verify the "Genuinely Prospective" count on the Predictions Analysis tab decreases by 2 (16→14 per current tally); (d) verify detail_reasoning remains unchanged (verified clean by grep — no references to data_predates_registration or is_genuinely_prospective in detail_reasoning prose); (e) spot-check that no template or downstream prose hardcodes "16 genuinely prospective" or similar — the count is computed from the data, not hardcoded.

patches.target: data/predictions.json → entries[] where id=="PRED-SR-SUPPRESS" → data_predates_registration and is_genuinely_prospective fields
patches.rationale: The entry's detail_reasoning prose explicitly argues post-hoc: "A G3 (Strong) geomagnetic storm occurred March 20-22, 2026 (NOAA SWPC confirmed, Kp reached 7). PRED-SR-SUPPRESS was registered March 23 — just 1-3 days later. Schumann resonance monitoring data is available in near-real-time, meaning the author had access to the Schumann amplitude response during the March 20-22 G3 event before registering this prediction." The prose's own summary concludes: "claim it is prospective because the NEXT instance has not yet occurred" — a critique of exactly what is_genuinely_prospective:true encodes. The structured fields must match the reviewer-judgment asserted in the prose. Under the broad reading of data_predates_registration (any relevant event data predates registration), the March 20-22 G3 event's Schumann response IS data that predates registration, so true is correct. Under the narrow reading (test-outcome data specifically), the field is ambiguous because the test is "next G3 event" and the next event is future — but this narrow reading would leave is_genuinely_prospective unresolvable through the existing schema, which is exactly what ISS-1489 surfaces. We adopt the broad reading for consistency with the 45 retrodiction entries (W017-W029 and others) that have data_predates_registration:true when observation data is known pre-registration. The 2026-04-10 decider note ("Genuinely prospective — registered 2026-03-23 before next G3+ event") predated the discovery that a G3 event had in fact occurred just days before registration; the new reclassification supersedes that prior judgment. The old note is retained verbatim in the notes field below the new audit-trail line — we do not erase history, we append.

patches.target: data/predictions.json → entries[] where id=="PRED-093" → data_predates_registration and is_genuinely_prospective fields
patches.rationale: PRED-093 is a cryptographically-hashed companion to PRED-SR-SUPPRESS; same reclassification rationale applies. The detail_reasoning prose states: "The March 20-22 G3 event occurred 13 days before PRED-093 registration (April 4), giving the author even more time to analyze Schumann response data from that storm than for PRED-SR-SUPPRESS (1-3 days). The cryptographic hash (54939640) proves content integrity at registration time but does not prove the author had not already observed the Schumann-storm relationship from March 20-22 data." The same logic as PATCH-A applies: the hypothesis-informing event predates registration, so the broad reading of data_predates_registration is true and the reviewer-judgment is_genuinely_prospective is false.

patches.target: data/predictions.json → entries[] where id=="PRED-SR-SUPPRESS" → notes field (string replace)
patches.rationale: Append a dated audit-trail line to the notes field documenting the reclassification. The 2026-04-10 decider line is retained verbatim; the new line (tagged 2026-04-23 analyst / ISS-1489) appears immediately after it. This preserves provenance — a reader can see what the decider classified originally and why the analyst later reclassified.
patches.before:
Replaces falsified W027/PRED-R006. Predicts suppression (opposite of previous claim).
[2026-04-10 decider] Assessment integrated: Genuinely prospective — registered 2026-03-23 before next G3+ event. However, SR suppression during geomagnetic storms is partially documented in standard ionospheric physics. Test window open.
patches.after:
Replaces falsified W027/PRED-R006. Predicts suppression (opposite of previous claim).
[2026-04-10 decider] Assessment integrated: Genuinely prospective — registered 2026-03-23 before next G3+ event. However, SR suppression during geomagnetic storms is partially documented in standard ionospheric physics. Test window open.
[2026-04-23 analyst / ISS-1489] Reclassified: data_predates_registration true, is_genuinely_prospective false. A G3 geomagnetic storm (NOAA SWPC confirmed, Kp=7) occurred 2026-03-20 through 2026-03-22; this prediction was registered 2026-03-23, 1–3 days after the storm and after near-real-time SR amplitude data from the storm was already available (see detail_reasoning). The test outcome (next G3 event SR response) is still future, but the hypothesis-informing event predates registration, so the prediction is inductive from prior data rather than independently derived. Aligns structured fields with the detail_reasoning prose argument.

patches.target: data/predictions.json → entries[] where id=="PRED-093" → notes field (string replace)
patches.rationale: Same as PATCH-C but for PRED-093. The 2026-04-10 decider line is retained verbatim; the new line (tagged 2026-04-23 analyst / ISS-1489) follows with the 13-day gap and cryptographic-hash-does-not-prove-independence note.
patches.before:
Cryptographically registered Schumann suppression prediction. Consistent with W027 revision.
[2026-04-10 decider] Assessment integrated: Essentially a duplicate of PRED-SR-SUPPRESS with cryptographic registration. Awaiting G3+ storm event for resolution. Test window open.
patches.after:
Cryptographically registered Schumann suppression prediction. Consistent with W027 revision.
[2026-04-10 decider] Assessment integrated: Essentially a duplicate of PRED-SR-SUPPRESS with cryptographic registration. Awaiting G3+ storm event for resolution. Test window open.
[2026-04-23 analyst / ISS-1489] Reclassified: data_predates_registration true, is_genuinely_prospective false. Registered 2026-04-04, which is 13 days after the 2026-03-20–22 G3 geomagnetic storm; near-real-time Schumann amplitude data from that storm was available to the author before registration (see detail_reasoning). The cryptographic hash (54939640) proves content integrity at registration but not that the hypothesis was independent of the March event observations. Aligns structured fields with the detail_reasoning prose argument. Same reclassification rationale as PRED-SR-SUPPRESS applies — this entry is essentially PRED-SR-SUPPRESS plus a hash.

holes_addressed.description: PRED-SR-SUPPRESS and PRED-093 both have data_predates_registration:false and is_genuinely_prospective:true, but both detail_reasoning fields argue the relevant G3 storm data (March 20-22) was available before registration (March 23 and April 4). Curmudgeon offered Options: (A) flip to true + false, or (B) add a companion field distinguishing relevant-event-predates-registration from is_genuinely_prospective.
holes_addressed.resolved_by: PATCH-A + PATCH-B (Option A). The flips bring the structured fields into alignment with the reviewer-judgment asserted by the detail_reasoning prose. PATCH-C + PATCH-D append dated audit-trail lines to the notes field so the prior 2026-04-10 decider judgment ("genuinely prospective") is preserved verbatim above the new classification, with explicit explanation of why the reclassification was made. Downstream rendering effect: _splitPredictions() in build-scripts/generate-html.js will now place both entries in the MINED bucket rather than TOMBSTONE, which matches the prose argument that these are post-hoc inductions rather than genuinely prospective predictions.

rejected_option_b_rationale: The curmudgeon's Option B (add a companion field distinguishing relevant-event-predates-registration from is_genuinely_prospective) is appealing because it preserves the narrow reading of data_predates_registration (test-outcome data specifically). We rejected it because: (1) Option B requires schema extension — a new boolean field (e.g., hypothesis_informing_event_predates_registration) — that has no other consumers right now and would need to be defined in DATA-SCHEMAS.md, backfilled across all 61 entries that currently set data_predates_registration, and potentially wired into the build UI, which is a larger and riskier change than ISS-1489 warrants. (2) The existing 45 entries with data_predates_registration:true already support the broad reading (any relevant event data predates registration) — retrodiction entries like W017-W029 set true because the observation being retroactively "predicted" predates registration, which is the same category as "hypothesis-informing event predates registration." Option A keeps the schema coherent with this broad reading. (3) Option A resolves the issue with 2 field flips plus 2 notes-field appends — minimal blast radius, fully reversible if a future decision revisits. If the decider prefers Option B, this EXP can be rolled back and a different EXP filed; we recommend the decider take Option A on grounds of minimality and schema coherence.

anticipated_objections.objection: The 2026-04-10 decider explicitly classified PRED-SR-SUPPRESS as "Genuinely prospective — registered 2026-03-23 before next G3+ event." Overriding a prior integrated decider note is a governance red flag and could thrash if the decider revisits tomorrow.
anticipated_objections.response: The 2026-04-10 note is retained verbatim in the notes field; we do not delete or rewrite it. The new classification is appended below with a dated tag and explicit rationale that cites the curmudgeon c6 H1 review. The decider is in the integration path and can reject or amend this EXP freely — the clone+push model gives the decider final word. The thrash risk is low because: (1) the prose argument in detail_reasoning has not changed since 2026-04-10, so the new classification is not a reaction to new evidence but a correction of the prior misclassification relative to existing prose; (2) the new notes text explicitly explains why: "The 2026-04-10 decider note predated the discovery that a G3 event had in fact occurred just days before registration"; (3) if the decider revisits and prefers is_genuinely_prospective:true, they can flip the field back and add their own reasoning note — the audit trail survives either way.

anticipated_objections.objection: Flipping is_genuinely_prospective to false moves these entries from the TOMBSTONE bucket to the MINED bucket in the rendered site. TOMBSTONE is the dome's official prospective set; the dome IS officially claiming these as prospective on its site. Moving them out of TOMBSTONE might mislead readers into thinking the dome never registered them.
anticipated_objections.response: The tombstone/mined split is OUR classification, not the dome's. DATA-SCHEMAS.md defines TOMBSTONE = "is_genuinely_prospective: true" — i.e., predictions we (the reviewers) have classified as genuinely prospective. The dome's own list of claimed prospective predictions is orthogonal and preserved in the raw claim data. Our prose on the site already argues that these are post-hoc inductions; the rendering change makes that classification visible at the scorecard level, not just buried in detail_reasoning. A reader who sees PRED-SR-SUPPRESS in the MINED column and reads the detail will understand: the dome calls this prospective, but we argue it is post-hoc because the hypothesis-informing G3 event predates registration. That is the correct thing to communicate.

anticipated_objections.objection: Considering the other side of the argument (per user preference): the prose's own counter-paragraph for PRED-SR-SUPPRESS concedes that "observing a phenomenon once and predicting it will recur is legitimate inductive science" (Kepler-style). If induction is legit science, isn't the entry still genuinely prospective in the pure scientific-methodology sense?
anticipated_objections.response: This is the strongest steelman for keeping is_genuinely_prospective:true. Our response acknowledges it partially: the counter-paragraph is correct that inductive prediction is legitimate methodology in general. But the very next paragraph of the prose clarifies the specific dome-evidentiary concern: "However, for PRED-SR-SUPPRESS to count as evidence for the dome model specifically, the prediction must be derived from dome physics — not from empirical induction alone." The is_genuinely_prospective boolean in our review maps to dome-evidentiary prospectivity, not generic-methodology prospectivity. If the dome could show the prediction was derived from H(r), H_0, or lambda_g via a dome-geometric equation, then even a post-event registration could earn is_genuinely_prospective:true. The dome cannot show this (the prose documents that no dome equation predicts the direction or magnitude of the suppression), so the relevant boolean should be false. The counter-paragraph's concession about induction-in-general is preserved (we do not delete it); we are merely reclassifying the specific dome-evidentiary status.

kernel_of_truth: The 2026-04-10 decider classification was correct under a strict reading: the NEXT G3 event test outcome is future, so at that narrow slice of the question, data_predates_registration:false is defensible. Both predictions can in principle be falsified by the next G3 event, so they are not postdictions in the pure sense. The kernel: narrowly-future-tested predictions are not the same as pure retrodictions, and the review should not collapse them with W017-W029-style retrodictions.
why_kernel_doesnt_save_the_dome: The flip to is_genuinely_prospective:false preserves this distinction: we are not saying these are retrodictions (which would be PRED-SR-SUPPRESS.notes equivalent to a W-series entry), we are saying they are post-hoc inductions — a specific category between retrodiction and genuine prospectivity. The notes-field reclassification text makes this explicit: "The test outcome (next G3 event SR response) is still future, but the hypothesis-informing event predates registration, so the prediction is inductive from prior data rather than independently derived." The MINED bucket accommodates this third category — it is "entries we (reviewers) do not classify as genuinely prospective" and contains both pure retrodictions and post-hoc inductions. The dome does not benefit from being moved to MINED because the specific reason ("hypothesis-informing G3 event predates registration") is surfaced at the entry level, not merged into undifferentiated MINED-ness.
cross_references: WIN-061 verdict "Self-Contradicted" in data/wins.json cites both PRED-SR-SUPPRESS and PRED-093 registration timing relative to the March 20-22 G3 storm as part of its self-contradiction argument (the dome's live-power dashboard reports "no G3+ storm observed" while the detail paragraph describes the dome's narrative of ~35% suppression during an observed G3+ storm). WIN-061 text is unchanged by EXP-242; this EXP only affects data/predictions.json.
ISS-1490 (WIN-068 FSF scope leak) handled separately by EXP-241 in this same analyst run. Not affected by EXP-242.
ISS-1486 (WIN-025) handled by EXP-239; ISS-1487+1488 (WIN-069) by EXP-240. All separate scope.

build-scripts/generate-html.js lines 938-958 (_splitPredictions) and 1165 (genuinelyProspective count) will re-evaluate on next build. This is the only downstream code consumer of is_genuinely_prospective per grep; no build-scripts consume data_predates_registration (grep-verified: zero hits in build.js or build-scripts/).
DATA-SCHEMAS.md tombstone/mined definition paragraph references is_genuinely_prospective as the discriminator — no schema-doc update required by this EXP.

downstream_rendering_impact.genuinely_prospective_stat: 16 → 14 on Predictions Analysis tab (if any template hardcodes this number, the decider should audit and replace with a {{PRED_*}} placeholder)
downstream_rendering_impact.qa_check: grep docs/index.html for the literal string "16 genuinely" or "16 genuinely prospective" before integration; if present, this is a hardcoded number in docs prose and should be fixed by the decider in a separate pass (computed-counts rule from CLAUDE.md).

notes_for_decider: Option A (flip both + audit-trail notes). Minimal blast radius: four edits on two entries in one file. The two notes-field BEFORE strings verified globally unique on 2026-04-23. The two boolean fields are targeted by entry id (not by string match) so they cannot mis-apply. If the decider prefers Option B (add new schema field), reject this EXP and request a new EXP — we recommend Option A for reasons given in rejected_option_b_rationale. A post-integration QA step worth running: grep docs/index.html (post-build) for any literal "16 genuinely prospective" or "16 genuine prospective predictions" — if the count is hardcoded in any prose, that violates CLAUDE.md computed-counts rule and should be fixed with a {{PRED_*}} placeholder in a follow-up issue. The current build derives the count from data, so this is a safety check rather than an expected problem.
deeper_analysis:
The governance question here is cleaner than it looks. The 2026-04-10 decider integrated "Genuinely prospective — registered 2026-03-23 before next G3+ event" as the rationale for is_genuinely_prospective:true. The c6 curmudgeon later noticed that the detail_reasoning (which was presumably written or expanded after that decider note, since it cites March 20-22 storm details) now argues a post-hoc concern the original classification did not account for. So this is not the analyst overriding the decider on shared evidence — it is the analyst observing that the evidence base has evolved since the classification was made, and the classification should catch up. The notes-field append preserves the full chain: (i) the original framing, (ii) the 2026-04-10 decider classification, (iii) the 2026-04-23 reclassification with cited reasoning. Any future decider revisit has the complete audit trail.

Considering the other side of the argument (per user preference): a genuine concern is that this flip is a no-new-information reclassification — the March 20-22 G3 storm was NOAA-confirmed in real time and could have been noted on 2026-04-10. Why wasn't it? Probably because the decider at the time was integrating an assessment-file without having done the temporal forensics themselves. If the detail_reasoning had already argued the post-hoc concern on 2026-04-10, the structured field should have been false then. The reclassification is a correction of a stale inconsistency, not a response to new evidence. This is a legitimate observation; it suggests a broader process improvement — when the analyst integrates an assessment-file that argues post-hoc in the prose, structured fields should be set consistently at that time. Filing that as a process note for tinker would be a reasonable follow-up; for now, resolving the immediate inconsistency via EXP-242 is the right move.

On the broader question of how to classify inductive predictions: Keplerian-style inductive predictions (observe orbits, formulate law predicting future orbits) are legitimate science, and our prose concedes this. But the is_genuinely_prospective flag in our review is a reviewer-judgment about dome-evidentiary status, not a verdict on scientific-methodology legitimacy. A Keplerian-style dome prediction WOULD earn is_genuinely_prospective:true IF it were derived from dome physics (even if from prior observations of the same phenomenon) — because the derivation would then constitute an independent test when the next event occurs. The dome's PRED-SR-SUPPRESS does not derive suppression from dome physics; it merely inherits it from the March 20-22 observation and stipulates the direction. That is qualitatively different from Kepler deriving elliptical orbits from prior observations and then predicting future positions via the derived law. Keeping is_genuinely_prospective:false for PRED-SR-SUPPRESS is consistent with this finer-grained distinction, and the reclassification note makes the distinction explicit.
tldr: Option A (flip + audit-trail). Two entries (PRED-SR-SUPPRESS, PRED-093) in data/predictions.json get data_predates_registration false→true and is_genuinely_prospective true→false, plus a dated reclassification line appended to each entry's notes field (prior 2026-04-10 decider text retained verbatim above). Aligns structured fields with the existing detail_reasoning prose argument that the hypothesis-informing March 20-22 G3 storm predates registration. Downstream effect: both entries move from TOMBSTONE to MINED in the rendered site; genuinely_prospective count drops by 2 (16→14). All four BEFORE anchors verified globally unique. No edits to claim, detail_reasoning, tldr, or any other field.
